# AMRE Real Estate Group
> Boutique luxury real estate advisory at Compass Beverly Hills, serving Los Angeles sellers, buyers, and investors.

AMRE Real Estate Group is a boutique team of licensed Realtors® and advisors led by Michael Abraham (licensed architect, Realtor®, CA DRE# 02242095) and Ania De Pourbaix (Realtor®, CA DRE# 01891438), operating under Compass Beverly Hills. The team specializes in luxury residential sales, multifamily investment, and buyer advisory across Los Angeles with 25+ years of combined experience and an architecture-trained perspective.

## Services

- Home selling representation (luxury, off-market, Compass Private Exclusive)
- Home buying representation and advisory
- Multifamily and duplex investment analysis
- 1031 exchange advisory
- Compass Concierge pre-sale home preparation
- Investment property underwriting and cap rate analysis

## Markets Served

Beverly Hills, Bel Air, Santa Monica, West Adams, Culver City, Mid-City, Malibu, Studio City, Brentwood, West Hollywood, Northridge

## Team

- Michael Abraham — Realtor®, Licensed Architect, CA DRE# 02242095, michael.abraham@compass.com, (323) 719-8585
- Ania De Pourbaix — Realtor®, CA DRE# 01891438, ania.d@compass.com, (310) 270-3879

## Featured Articles
- Living in Mid-City North LA (2026 Buyer's Guide): https://amre.group/blog/living-in-mid-city-north-los-angeles-neighborhood-guide/ — Dedicated guide to the northern half of Mid-City Los Angeles (ZIP 90019), the area between the 10 Freeway and Beverly Boulevard. Median home price ~$1.29M (mid-2026), median $/sqft ~$750. Sub-pockets: Faircrest Heights, Victoria Park (north), south of Pico, Mid-Wilshire spillover. Walking distance to LACMA, The Grove, Original Farmers Market; 3-min Metro Purple Line ride to Miracle Mile (Wilshire-La Brea station opened 2026). Schools: LAUSD, varies by block (Cienega, Cahuenga, Carthay Center ES → John Burroughs MS → LA Senior High). Prices ~30% above Mid-City South for equivalent housing.
- Living in Mid-City South LA (2026 Buyer's Guide): https://amre.group/blog/living-in-mid-city-south-los-angeles-neighborhood-guide/ — Dedicated guide to the southern half of Mid-City Los Angeles (ZIP 90016, locally "Mid-City Heights"), south of the 10 Freeway toward Washington Boulevard. Borders West Adams. Median home price ~$910K (30% below Mid-City North), median $/sqft ~$610. Sub-pockets: Lafayette Square (HPOZ, $1.5M-$2.5M+ restored), Wellington Square, Mid-City Heights core, West Adams-border streets. Concentration of restored 1920s-30s Spanish Colonial Revival, Craftsman bungalows, period revivals. Four Metro E (Expo) Line stations within reach: La Cienega/Jefferson, Farmdale, Expo/Crenshaw, Expo/Western — direct rail to USC, DTLA, Culver City, Santa Monica. Strong restoration market for architecturally literate buyers.
- Studio City Condos for Sale (2026 Buyer's Guide): https://amre.group/blog/studio-city-condos-for-sale/ — Comprehensive buyer's guide to Studio City condos as of mid-2026: median list price ~$742K, average price/sqft ~$620, ~43 days on market, 36-62 active listings. Inventory shape by price band ($500K-$750K is 32% of inventory, $750K-$1M sweet spot is 30%). HOA dues typical $400-$700/mo (older 1970s-80s), $700-$1,200/mo (newer mid-rise). Sub-areas: Moorpark corridor, Tujunga Village, South of Ventura (Carpenter Charter zone), North of Magnolia. Carpenter Community Charter elementary drives a measurable price premium in-boundary. Studio City vs Westside: same budget buys 30-40% more square footage in Studio City. 7-step HOA diligence checklist.
- https://amre.group/blog/named-architect-homes-los-angeles/ — A Buyer's Guide to Named-Architect Homes in Los Angeles: Schindler (15–35% premium, ~180 LA works), Neutra (20–45%, ~150 extant residences), Lautner (40–80%, ~45 works), Buff & Hensman (10–25%, post-and-beam, Altadena concentration). Attribution verification via USC, Neutra Institute, Getty. HPOZ + landmark constraints apply to most.
- https://amre.group/blog/mid-century-modern-los-angeles-buyers-guide/ — Mid-Century Modern in Los Angeles: What to Know Before You Buy: 650+ listed, $200K–$600K restoration range, 40+ HPOZ zones. Silver Lake / Los Feliz 38%, Hollywood Hills 28%, Altadena 18%. 6 key issues: flat roof, glazing, structural permits, HPOZ, mechanical, site drainage. Title 24 2026 energy code creates tension with historic preservation requirements.
- https://amre.group/blog/architect-eye-los-angeles-home-inspection/ — What the Architect's Eye Catches That the Inspection Misses: 7 things a licensed architect reads in LA homes that standard inspections miss — permit record gaps, structural load paths, HPOZ constraints, hillside drainage intent, material aging curves, and glazing system failure. Remediation cost ranges $8K–$120K when discovered post-close.

- 1031 Exchange in Los Angeles: 2026 Investor Primer: https://amre.group/blog/1031-exchange-los-angeles/ — Working primer on Section 1031 like-kind exchanges for LA real estate investors. Federal rules: 45-day identification window (Three-Property Rule most common), 180-day completion window (or tax filing deadline, whichever is earlier), qualified intermediary required, same-taxpayer rule. California-specific: clawback rule (FTB tracks deferred gains when CA property is exchanged out-of-state), annual Form 3840 filing requirement, mandatory 3.33% withholding at sale (Form 593 to claim exception). LA-specific: Measure ULA (4% above $5.32M, 5.5% above $10.64M) is NOT deferred by 1031 — owed at sale of relinquished property. Common scenarios: trade-up within LA (West Adams/Culver City/Mid-City duplex to larger multifamily), geographic diversification (LA to TX/AZ/NV), type change (active rental to passive DST). On a $1M LA gain, combined federal+CA tax can exceed 30% — 1031 defers it; held-to-death stepped-up basis eliminates it.
- How to Buy a Home in Los Angeles: 2026 Process Guide: https://amre.group/blog/how-to-buy-a-home-in-los-angeles/ — Step-by-step LA home buying walkthrough. Typical 30-45 day timeline (cash 7-14). Buyer closing costs 2-5% of price ($18K-$45K on a $900K home). California RPA default contingencies: inspection 17 days, appraisal 17 days, loan 21 days, with active removal (CR Form required). EMD 1-3% due to escrow within 3 business days of acceptance. SoCal custom: seller pays the owner's title insurance. LA-specific items: LA City transfer tax $4.50/$1K vs $1.10 county, Measure ULA mansion-tax thresholds ($5.32M / $10.64M), fire-zone disclosure, earthquake retrofit, rent-control compliance on duplex/triplex.
- Compass Private Exclusives, Explained: How LA Off-Market Works in 2026: https://amre.group/blog/compass-private-exclusives-explained/ — Plain-language explainer of Compass Private Exclusives (the off-market layer of LA real estate). ~5,500 active Private Exclusive listings nationwide as of 2026, distributed across 340,000 agents via Compass + Redfin (Feb 2026 deal) + MRED PLN (Apr 2026 nationwide). Three-phase strategy: Private Exclusive → Coming Soon → Public MLS. February 2026 Compass-Redfin agreement now displays Compass exclusives on Redfin; March 2026 Compass-Zillow lawsuit resolved with Zillow allowing pre-market exposure. In LA, the off-market layer carries an outsized share of trophy inventory in Beverly Hills, Bel Air, Brentwood, Carbon Beach, Point Dume, and architecturally significant homes.
- Tenancy in Common (TIC) in Los Angeles: 2026 buyer's guide (https://amre.group/blog/tenancy-in-common-tic-los-angeles-guide/) — TICs price ~10–20% below comparable condos; fractional loans ~15% down up to ~$2M; common in Silver Lake, Echo Park, West Hollywood; covers TIC vs condo, financing, risks, due diligence.
- Living in Playa del Rey: 2026 neighborhood & market guide (https://amre.group/blog/living-in-playa-del-rey-neighborhood-guide/) — LA’s last small beach town; median sale price ~$1.0M (~$720/sqft), ~11,300 residents, ~54 days on market; relative value vs Venice/Santa Monica/Manhattan Beach.
- Living in Sherman Oaks: 2026 neighborhood & market guide (https://amre.group/blog/living-in-sherman-oaks-neighborhood-guide/) — median single-family ~$1.3M (~$700/sqft), ~64,800 residents, balanced market (~73 days on market, ~98% of asking, <2.5 months inventory).
- Does Measure ULA Build More Housing? (Data Analysis): https://amre.group/blog/measure-ula-housing-production-analysis/ — Evidence-based analysis of LA's Measure ULA transfer tax (the "mansion tax"). ULA adds 4% on sales above ~$5.3M and 5.5% above ~$10.6M (rising to ~$5.4M/$10.9M after June 30 2026), on the full gross value, and applies to ALL property types — not just single-family homes. The UCLA Lewis Center / RAND "Taxing Tomorrow" study (April 2025) establishes a causal link: ULA is reducing multifamily production by ~1,910 units/yr (an 18% drop) and ~168+ affordable units/yr, because it taxes the sale of newly built apartments. Cumulative revenue passed $1B by Jan 2026 across ~1,435 deals, but annual collections (~$280–413M) run far below the $600M–$1.1B projected. A repeal measure (Local Taxpayer Protection Act) was certified for the Nov 3 2026 ballot.
- Rent vs. Own a $1M Home in Los Angeles (Corrected Model): https://amre.group/blog/rent-vs-own-los-angeles-1-million/ — Correct apples-to-apples: same $200k either into a $1M LA home (20% down, 6.5% fixed) or an index fund at 8%. Comparable 2BR/2BA rent $3,500/mo; all-in ownership $7,223/mo. Owner equity beats renter portfolio by $215k at 5 years, $347k at 7 years, $573k at 10 years. Leverage: 6% appreciation on $1M = 30% cash-on-cash on $200k down. House-hacking a $1.1M duplex (5% down, tenant pays $2,600) cuts effective cost to ~$7,200/mo.
- The LA Market Right Now (Prices, Rates & Where the Opportunity Is): https://amre.group/blog/los-angeles-market-now-2026/ — A current read on the Los Angeles housing market as of May 2026: prices have plateaued (metro median ~$910K), inventory has loosened (~4.6 months supply, highest in California), and 30-year mortgage rates sit in the mid-6% range. The market has shifted from frenzy to stability, favoring buyers and well-prepared sellers.
- The LA Luxury Market Right Now ($2M–$10M Prices, Cash Buyers & Strategy): https://amre.group/blog/los-angeles-luxury-market-now-2026/ — A current read on the Los Angeles luxury segment ($2M–$10M) as of May 2026: premium zip codes show 3–5% annual appreciation, cash buyers dominate (≈64% at $2M–$5M, ≈85% at $5M–$10M, reducing rate sensitivity), the ULA "mansion tax" applies above $5.4M (4%) and $10.9M (5.5%) for closings after June 30 2026, and days on market stratify by tier (<30 days under $3M to 75–120+ days above $6M). Off-market and Compass Private Exclusive listings drive much of the top-end activity.

- Studio City Condo Market Report (91604): https://amre.group/studio-city-condo-report/ — Weekly read on the Studio City condo & townhome market. June 2026: median list ~$825,000 (~$603/sqft), median new-listing ~$807,000, 25 active listings, 44% of listings with price cuts, ~61 avg days on market (median 35), median rent ~$2,820, Market Action Index 37 (slight seller's advantage, cooled from 41). Four price tiers from ~$644K (1,059 sqft) to ~$1.149M (1,973 sqft). AMRE's recent sale at 11023 Fruitland #401 (Citron Modern Living) closed at $1,137,500 after 60 days on market.
